Title: Create New Contract
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contracts module installed and accessible
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Click Add button in toolbar
3. Fill in Name field (e.g. 'Test Contract Alpha')
4. Select Status from dropdown (Draft)
5. Optionally fill Description field
6. Set Start date and End date
7. Select Vendor from dropdown
8. Verify Store is locked to @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)
9. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Contracts list blade opens with Add button visible
- New contract blade opens with empty fields
- Code field is auto-generated and read-only
- Store field is pre-filled with @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) and not editable
- Vendor dropdown shows available vendors
- Status dropdown shows Active and Draft options
- Save button persists the contract
- New contract appears in the contracts list

Title: View Contract Details
Preconditions: Admin logged in, At least one contract exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Click on an existing contract in the list
Expected Result:
- Contract details blade opens
- All fields displayed: Name, Code, Status, Description, Start date, End date, Vendor, Store
- Widgets visible: Attachments (count), Customers (count), Dynamic properties (count), Prices (count)
- All data matches what was saved

Title: Edit Existing Contract
Preconditions: Admin logged in, At least one contract exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Click on an existing contract
3. Modify Name field
4. Change Status from Draft to Active
5. Update Description
6. Modify Start date and End date
7. Click Save
8. Close blade and reopen the contract
Expected Result:
- All fields are editable (except Code and Store)
- Changes are accepted in the form
- Save button commits changes
- Reopening the contract shows persisted updated values

Title: Delete Contract
Preconditions: Admin logged in, At least one deletable contract exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Select a contract using checkbox
3. Click Delete button in toolbar
4. Confirm deletion in dialog
Expected Result:
- Delete button becomes active when a contract is selected
- Confirmation dialog appears before deletion
- Contract is removed from the list after confirmation
- Deleted contract no longer accessible via direct navigation

Title: Search and Filter Contracts
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Multiple contracts exist (at least 3)
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Type a known contract name into the search field
3. Verify filtered results
4. Clear search field
5. Click Refresh button
Expected Result:
- Search field filters contracts list in real-time or on submit
- Only matching contracts are displayed
- Clearing search restores full list
- Refresh button reloads the contracts list without stale data

Title: Toggle Contract Status Active and Draft
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contract exists with Status = Draft
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Open a contract with Status = Draft
3. Change Status dropdown to Active
4. Click Save
5. Reopen the contract
6. Change Status back to Draft
7. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Status dropdown allows switching between Active and Draft
- Saved status persists after blade close and reopen
- No validation errors when toggling status

Title: Start Date Must Be Before End Date
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contract blade open for editing
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Open an existing contract or create new
3. Set Start date to 2026-06-01
4. Set End date to 2026-05-01 (before start date)
5. Click Save
Expected Result:
- System validates date range on save
- Error message displayed indicating End date must be after Start date
- Contract is not saved with invalid date range

Title: Expired Contract Behavior
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contract exists with End date in the past
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Open a contract with End date in the past
3. Verify displayed status and field states
4. Attempt to edit fields and save
Expected Result:
- Expired contract is visible in the list
- Contract details are viewable
- System either prevents editing or allows edit with a warning about expired dates
- Status reflects the contract's expired state appropriately

Title: Auto-Generated Code Format
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Click Add button
3. Fill required fields (Name, Status)
4. Observe the Code field before saving
5. Click Save
6. Create a second contract and observe its Code
Expected Result:
- Code field is auto-populated and read-only
- Code follows a consistent format pattern
- Each new contract receives a unique code
- Code does not change after save

Title: Add Attachment to Contract
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Open an existing contract
3. Click on Attachments widget
4. Upload a file (e.g. PDF or image)
5. Save the contract
6. Reopen the contract and check Attachments widget
Expected Result:
- Attachments widget opens a blade for file management
- File upload completes successfully
- Attachment count increments after upload
- Attachment persists after save and reopen
- Uploaded file is listed with correct name

Title: Assign Customer to Contract
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contract exists, At least one customer/contact exists in the system
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Open an existing contract
3. Click on Customers widget
4. Add a customer from the available list
5. Save the contract
6. Reopen the contract and verify Customers widget
Expected Result:
- Customers widget opens a blade for customer assignment
- Customer can be selected and added to the contract
- Customer count increments after assignment
- Assigned customer persists after save and reopen

Title: Add Dynamic Property to Contract
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contract exists, Dynamic properties configured for Contract entity type
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Open an existing contract
3. Click on Dynamic properties widget
4. Add or fill in a dynamic property value
5. Save the contract
6. Reopen the contract and check Dynamic properties widget
Expected Result:
- Dynamic properties widget opens with configured properties
- Property values can be entered or selected
- Values persist after save
- Dynamic property count reflects the number of properties with values

Title: Add Contract-Specific Price
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contract exists, Products exist in catalog
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Open an existing contract
3. Click on Prices widget
4. Add a price entry for a specific product
5. Set price amount and currency
6. Save the contract
7. Reopen the contract and verify Prices widget
Expected Result:
- Prices widget opens a blade for contract pricing
- Product can be selected for pricing
- Price amount and currency fields are editable
- Price entry persists after save and reopen
- Price count increments accordingly

Title: Create Contract Without Name - Validation
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Click Add button
3. Leave Name field empty
4. Fill other fields (Status, dates)
5. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Save button is either disabled when Name is empty or Save triggers a validation error
- Error message indicates Name is required
- Contract is not created without a name

Title: Duplicate Code Validation
Preconditions: Admin logged in, At least one contract exists with a known Code
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Click Add button
3. Observe the auto-generated Code
4. If Code is editable: manually enter a Code that already exists and try to Save
5. If Code is read-only: verify two consecutively created contracts receive different codes
Expected Result:
- If Code is editable: system rejects duplicate codes with an error message
- If Code is auto-generated and read-only: each contract gets a unique code
- No two contracts can share the same Code value

Title: End Date Before Start Date Rejected
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Click Add button
3. Fill Name field
4. Set End date to 2026-01-01
5. Set Start date to 2026-12-31
6. Click Save
Expected Result:
- System validates that End date cannot be before Start date
- Validation error is displayed to the user
- Contract is not saved with invalid date configuration

Title: Contract Pricing Applies to Assigned Customer
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contract exists with a customer assigned and contract-specific price set for a product, Storefront accessible
Steps:
1. Open the contract and note the assigned customer and the product with contract price
2. Log in to the storefront as the assigned customer
3. Navigate to the product with contract-specific pricing
4. Verify the displayed price matches the contract price
5. Add product to cart and verify cart price
Expected Result:
- Product price on storefront reflects the contract-specific price for the assigned customer
- Cart shows the contract price, not the default catalog price
- Non-assigned customers see the default price for the same product

Title: Vendor Assignment Links to Contact
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Contract exists, Vendors/contacts exist in the system
Steps:
1. Navigate to More > Contracts
2. Open an existing contract
3. Select a Vendor from the dropdown
4. Save the contract
5. Navigate to the Vendor/Contact module and locate the selected vendor
6. Verify the vendor record reflects the contract association
Expected Result:
- Vendor dropdown lists available vendors/contacts
- Selected vendor is saved with the contract
- Vendor selection persists after save and reopen
- Vendor entity is properly linked to the contract in the system
